---
title: "MAD213 Basic Statistics, Week 01" 
description: "Lecture: Introduction"
date: 2020-10-09T00:01:00-03:00
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
        collapsed: true
        smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    fig_caption: yes
    code_folding: show
    number_sections: true

fontsize: 14pt
tags: [mad213_lecturenotes,mad213_lecturenotes_week01]
comments: false
---
```{r , echo=FALSE}
library(kableExtra)
text_tbl <- data.frame(
Date=c("October 9"),
Topic=c("Introduction")
)

kbl(text_tbl,escape = F) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = T)%>%
  column_spec(1, width = "8em")%>%
    column_spec(2, width = "40em")%>%
  row_spec(1, background = "orange", color="black")

```

Dear all,

Textbook recommendation for the MAD213 are as follows: 

1. **Online Statistics Education: An Interactive Multimedia Course of Study**, Developed by Rice University , University of Houston Clear Lake, and Tufts University. [[pdf link]](http://onlinestatbook.com/Online_Statistics_Education.pdf) (In lecture, I will refer this open source pdf as ***"Online Statistics pdf"***)
2. Pierre Lafaye de Micheaux, Rémy Drouilhet, Benoit Liquet, 2013, "**The R Software Fundamentals of Programming and Statistical Analysis**", Springer
3. John Chambers, 2008, "**Software for Data Analysis**",
4. Jacek M. Czaplicki, 2014, "**Statistics for Mining Engineering**", CRC Press London

See you on October 16th via Zoom!

P.S. - Just have a look and read Chapter 1. Introduction from the ***"Online Statistics pdf"*** prior to next lecture.

`r emo::ji("sun")` GERTUNC `r emo::ji("bar_chart")`
